anomaly.definition: A large, city-based, cross-faction battle for control over a set window. The marquee Ingress event.

ap.definition: Access Points, your experience. Earned mostly by building, linking and fielding, and what levels you up.

banner.definition: A set of missions whose icons tile into a larger picture on your profile. Built in rows of six.

capsule.definition: A container holding up to 100 items, for sorting and trading gear. Contents still count toward your cap.

cmu.definition: Chaotic Matter Units, the in-game store currency.

comms.definition: The in-scanner chat, with a faction channel (your team) and an all channel (everyone in range).

control-field.definition: The filled triangle made by linking three of your portals together. Scores AP and claims Mind Units.

core.definition: The optional monthly subscription: a CMU grant, +500 inventory capacity, and a gear kit.

dispatch.definition: An in-scanner system that delivers events as Campaigns made of Bounties.

first-saturday.definition: A monthly local meetup (IngressFS), and the best place for a new agent to start.

flip-card.definition: An ADA Refactor or JARVIS Virus: a rare item that instantly flips a portal’s faction.

fracker.definition: Deploy one and every item hacked from that portal is duplicated, for all agents, for ten minutes.

glyph-hack.definition: Hacking by tracing a sequence of glyphs from memory, for bonus items and extra AP.

hack.definition: Tapping a portal to collect gear. Costs no XM, but limited by a cooldown and a burnout limit.

heat-sink.definition: A hack mod that cuts a portal’s cooldown and, when you deploy it, resets the timers for you.

hypercube.definition: A rare power cube that tops your XM back up automatically as you spend, until it is empty.

intel-map.definition: intel.ingress.com, a view-only desktop map of the whole game, useful for planning.

ito-transmuter.definition: A mod that biases a portal’s hack output toward offensive (ITO-) or defensive (ITO+) gear.

key.definition: Obtained by hacking a portal; needed to link to it. Can be stored in a key locker outside your item cap.

key-locker.definition: Storage that holds keys without counting toward your 2,000-item inventory cap.

kinetic-capsule.definition: A capsule that crafts gear as you walk a set distance, normally 8 km per Program.

level.definition: Agent levels run 1–16; portal levels run 1–8. Your level caps the level of gear you can use.

link.definition: A beam joining two of your fully-built portals. You need the destination portal’s key, and links cannot cross.

link-amp.definition: A mod that extends how far a portal can link out.

machina.definition: The red, machine-controlled faction. It spreads on its own and is easy to clear.

mind-unit.definition: The score a control field claims, roughly the population living under it. The global blue-versus-green tally.

mission.definition: A curated sequence of portal tasks that earns a unique medal icon on your profile.

mod.definition: An item slotted into a portal (shield, heat sink, link amp, and so on). Four slots per portal, two per agent.

multi-hack.definition: A hack mod that raises how many times a portal can be hacked before it burns out.

nl-1331.definition: The XM Detection Vehicle: an occasional touring van, and now also a virtual event run through Dispatch.

portal.definition: A real-world point of interest you visit to play. Colored gray (neutral), blue (Resistance), green (Enlightened) or red (Machina).

power-cube.definition: Bottled XM you tap to refill instantly. Levels 1–8 hold 1,000–8,000 XM.

recursion.definition: Choosing at level 16 to reset to level 1, keeping all your badges. A prestige system.

resonator.definition: An item you deploy to capture and power a portal. Eight slots per portal; their average sets the portal level.

sbul.definition: A rare linking mod: +5x link range and +8 outbound links per copy, used to anchor big fielding ops.

scanner.definition: The Ingress app itself, your window into the game.

second-sunday.definition: The monthly online counterpart to First Saturday, run through Dispatch.

shield.definition: A defense mod that adds mitigation, reducing the damage a portal’s resonators take when attacked.

ultra-strike.definition: A focused, short-range weapon, used to strip mods and shields and finish stubborn resonators.

wayfarer.definition: Niantic’s system for reviewing new portal nominations. It replaced the old OPR.

xm.definition: Exotic Matter, the energy that powers your actions. You collect it by walking through it.

xmp-burster.definition: Your main weapon. It detonates in a circle centered on you, damaging enemy resonators.
